hyperedges.label: Visual Companion implementation stack
hyperedges.rationale: Six artifacts together constitute the Visual Companion subsystem: spec (SKILL.md + visual-companion.md), presentation (frame-template.html), client (helper.js), and lifecycle (start-server.sh / stop-server.sh).

hyperedges.label: Six bugs in docs/bug-fix-spec.md
hyperedges.rationale: Bug spec enumerates six findings pinned by failing Jest tests across src/indexer.ts, src/sandbox/executor.ts, and src/backends/detect.ts.

hyperedges.label: Critical security findings (F-01, F-02)
hyperedges.rationale: Two of four critical findings: no-op sandbox profile and missing LLM-output trust separation. Both are architectural defects rather than isolated bugs.

hyperedges.label: Superpowers Review Workflow (spec compliance + code quality + reception)
Superpowers Iron Law Disciplines (TDD + Debugging + Plans)
Self-Healing Repair Flow (classify -> circuit -> agent -> snapshot -> playbook)
F-26 replay security posture (dry-run + HMAC + sandboxed executor)
Error classification + post-mortem pipeline (origin → class → incident → playbook)
Spec-driven testing pipeline (parse -> classify -> synthesize -> format)
Requirement taxonomy (functional / edge / constraint / non-goal / bug / ambiguous)
Sandbox isolation invariants (path escape + exec sandboxed)

attributes.rationale: Defines build commands, 9router integration, project structure, CodeGraph usage, and Superpowers skill methodology for the 9rh repo.
Catalogues top-level modules (agent.ts, tools.ts, commands.ts, index.ts) and the public library surface.
Quick-start guide covering 9router and direct mode, backend auto-detection precedence.

attributes.purpose: Ad-hoc smoke test: instantiates Sandbox, runs `echo hello`, dumps profile+result.

attributes.rationale: Catalogue of security findings F-01..F-49 against the 9rh harness; identifies no-op macOS sandbox profile, missing trust separation, and 36 high-confidence prompt-injection sinks.
Three-phase plan for repo indexing: sync indexer (SQLite/WAL), background sub-agent, cross-session persistence.
Documents six bug fixes pinned by failing Jest tests, with required-fix sketches and commit-order recommendation.
Comprehensive CLI/REPL/programmatic reference including sandbox, replay, and repair system architecture.
Defines a 9-step brainstorming checklist with a hard gate that forbids implementation until design is approved.
Subagent prompt template for reviewing spec documents against Completeness, Consistency, Clarity, Scope, YAGNI.
Describes browser-based visual brainstorming companion (WebSocket events, content fragments, indicator bar).

attributes.purpose: Frame UI with OS-aware light/dark theming via prefers-color-scheme.
WebSocket client connecting to ws://window.location.host, captures [data-choice] clicks, exposes window.brainstorm API.
Launches node server.cjs with BRAINSTORM_DIR/HOST/OWNER_PID env vars; auto-foreground on Codex CI / MSYS.
Reads PID from PID_FILE, SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL; only deletes /tmp session dirs, keeps .superpowers/.

attributes.rationale: Root product concept: a streaming ReAct agent harness written in TypeScript/Node with backend abstraction, sandboxed tools, and a slash-command REPL.
Sidecar router on :20128 exposing OpenAI-compatible /v1/* and native /api/* endpoints; default backend for 9rh.
Pluggable Backend interface with 6-layer resolution precedence (flag, env, config, heuristic, probe, fallback).
sandboxPath() enforces workDir; macOS sandbox-exec profile with DirectExecutor fallback when profile fails.
Core agent control flow: iterative Thought→Action→Observation using the OpenAI client and TOOL_DEFINITIONS.
Sub-millisecond knowledge graph of symbols/edges/files; primary read tool for structural questions.
Separate ladybugdb-backed graph (1821 nodes, 3869 edges, 151 processes, 71 communities) with FTS but no vector search.
13-skill methodology (brainstorming, writing-plans, TDD, systematic-debugging, ...) loaded from skills/<name>/SKILL.md.
JSONL event log with divergence detection and branching; 7 modules under src/replay/.

nodes.label: Repair system (4 error classes + circuit breaker)
nodes.author: jergensturdley

attributes.rationale: 6 modules under src/repair/ implementing RECOVERABLE/AGENT_ERROR/ENVIRONMENT_ERROR/FATAL classification, playbook, and circuit breaker after 3 consecutive failures.
Browser-based brainstorm UI with content fragments, indicator bar, [data-choice] clicks, JSONL event stream.
REPL-wide state passed by reference; routerCache is session-scoped and reset by /refresh.
Multi-role pipeline under src/orchestrator/ (roles.ts, conflictResolver.ts, performanceCache.ts).
src/faultInjection/ module: injector + scenarios + evaluator + report + registry + types.
src/spec/specDrivenTesting.ts: parseTaskSpecification + formatSpecDrivenPrompt pipeline.
mkdir/writeFile rejections are caught and discarded, causing in-memory store to diverge from disk with no signal.
Returns input verbatim; /tmp/../etc/passwd resolves to /etc/passwd outside sandbox — sandbox boundary violation.
e.signal never read; SIGKILL indistinguishable from OOM kill from timeout.

nodes.label: Bug #5: DirectExecutor.exec clamps timeoutMs at 120_000 silently
nodes.author: jergensturdley

attributes.rationale: Caller asking for 10 minutes gets 2 minutes, no warning.
Production code is correct (.git skipped at every depth, .config carved out); docstring only needs update.
Boolean(env) OR (routerUrl !== DEFAULT) is tautological; second clause subsumes first. No runtime impact.

nodes.label: Security Finding F-01 (Critical): macOS sandbox profile no-op allow-all
nodes.author: jergensturdley

attributes.rationale: Sandbox profile reads `(version 1)(allow default)` — equivalent to no sandbox at all.

nodes.label: Security Finding F-02 (Critical): No trust separation between LLM output and input
nodes.author: jergensturdley

attributes.rationale: No untrusted envelope on LLM-produced text; tool args can be injected by prompt content.

nodes.label: Security Finding F-26 (High): Replay engine re-executes recorded tool calls
nodes.author: jergensturdley

attributes.rationale: Planted JSONL attack: attacker-supplied replay events cause execution of arbitrary tool calls during replay.

nodes.label: When to Use Parallel Agents (decision tree)
Agent Prompt Structure (focused, self-contained, specific output)
Three Step Process: Load, Execute, Complete
Stop and Ask for Help Discipline
Six Step Process: Verify, Detect, Present, Execute, Cleanup
Four Completion Options (Merge, PR, Keep, Discard)
Six Step Response Pattern (Read, Understand, Verify, Evaluate, Respond, Implement)
When To Push Back On Reviewer
Code Reviewer Output Format (Strengths, Issues, Recommendations, Assessment)
Two-Stage Review Per Task (spec compliance then code quality)
Four Implementer Statuses (DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, NEEDS_CONTEXT, BLOCKED)
Iron Law: No Fixes Without Root Cause Investigation
Four Phases (Root Cause, Pattern, Hypothesis, Implementation)
Red Flags - Stop and Follow Process
Iron Law: No Production Code Without Failing Test First

Scenario: Permission Denied on File Read

Signal-killed detection heuristic on success branch
Bug #4 — ExecutionResult signal preservation on kill
Spec-driven testing principle (test generation as first-class artifact, not side effect)
Tight implementation loop rationale (fail-test-first for explicit bugs)
Pause-on-ambiguity rationale (do not proceed broadly when ambiguities materially affect behavior)
